title: The Phantom Workforce of {city.name}
trigger: A routine payroll system update reveals thousands of city employees who haven't badged into a building since 2018.
description: An investigative piece by {city.media_outlet} uncovered that 15% of the sanitation department consists of deceased or fictional people. The {city.government_body} is feigning shock, despite several council members' relatives sharing names with the spectral workers.

options.label: Quietly purge the names and pay a small severance to the 'families'.

consequences.follow_up: The ghosts are laid to rest with a generous golden parachute. The budget bleeds, but the press gets bored.
consequences.city_reaction: Even the dead get better benefits than us. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Launch a loud, public anti-corruption task force.

consequences.follow_up: The task force accidentally implicates the deputy mayor. Several hard drives are mysteriously wiped with magnets.
consequences.city_reaction: Ah yes, investigating themselves. I'm sure they'll find the culprit. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Demand the ghost employees show up for work or face termination.

consequences.follow_up: Hundreds of random citizens show up claiming to be the ghosts to collect the back pay. Absolute administrative gridlock.
consequences.city_reaction: I am Spartacus, and I also work in sanitation. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Empty Vaults in {city.name}
trigger: Retired {city.primary_industry_workers} discover their monthly pension checks have bounced.
description: It turns out the {city.government_body} borrowed from the municipal pension fund to finance a beautification project near {city.landmark}. The money is gone, replaced by an IOU written on a napkin.

options.label: Take out a high-interest commercial loan to cover the shortfall.

consequences.follow_up: The checks clear, but the city's credit rating drops to junk. Our grandchildren will be paying this off.
consequences.city_reaction: Got my money, don't care if the city burns tomorrow. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Admit the theft and offer municipal bonds as compensation.

consequences.follow_up: The bonds are virtually worthless. Retirees start selling them on the street as novelty souvenirs.
consequences.city_reaction: Trading my retirement for a piece of paper with the mayor's face on it. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Claim a clerical error and freeze all payouts indefinitely.

consequences.follow_up: Seniors chain themselves to city hall doors. A riot police standoff with grandmothers ensues for three days.
consequences.city_reaction: Never thought I'd see my grandma tear-gassed, but here we are. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Invisible Roads of {city.name}
trigger: A newly completed highway extension is revealed to be just a dirt path with a single traffic cone.
description: Records show a shell company was paid millions to pave roads connecting the {city.primary_industry_workers} district. {city.media_outlet} reporters drove to the company's registered address and found a vacant lot.

options.label: Hire a real contractor to quickly do the job at double the cost.

consequences.follow_up: The road is built hastily and will probably crack in a year, but the immediate outrage subsides.
consequences.city_reaction: Only took two tries and triple the taxes to get a flat road. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Sue the shell company to recover the funds.

consequences.follow_up: The lawsuit costs more than the stolen funds. The CEO turns out to be a 90-year-old man with dementia.
consequences.city_reaction: Great job suing a grandpa. Real tough on crime. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Declare the dirt path an eco-friendly greenway and ban cars on it.

consequences.follow_up: Commuters are furious. The dirt path becomes a massive mud pit during the next rainstorm.
consequences.city_reaction: Returning to nature by destroying my suspension. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Two Sets of Books in {city.name}
trigger: A disgruntled accountant leaks the real municipal ledger to {city.media_outlet}.
description: The official budget is a work of fiction. The shadow ledger reveals exorbitant spending on luxury vehicles for the {city.government_body} and a suspicious slush fund labeled Miscellaneous Operations.

options.label: Settle with the federal prosecutors and pay a massive fine.

consequences.follow_up: The city avoids criminal charges by emptying its reserves. Austerity measures are quietly implemented.
consequences.city_reaction: We pay the fine for them stealing our money. Makes sense. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Claim the leaked ledger is a deepfake and launch a witch hunt.

consequences.follow_up: The whistleblower is arrested on trumped-up charges, sparking protests. The truth is buried, for now.
consequences.city_reaction: They really expect us to believe an Excel spreadsheet is CGI. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Legalize the slush fund retroactively by passing an emergency ordinance.

consequences.follow_up: The brazen move shocks even cynical observers. International transparency watchdogs downgrade the city immediately.
consequences.city_reaction: If you can't beat the law, just change it. Classic. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The $500 Lightbulbs of {city.name}
trigger: An intern accidentally publishes the procurement receipts for city hall renovations.
description: Citizens are outraged to learn the city paid astronomical prices for basic goods. The supplier is conveniently owned by the brother-in-law of the head of the {city.government_body}.

options.label: Cancel the remaining orders and quietly switch suppliers.

consequences.follow_up: The new supplier is only slightly less overpriced. The brother-in-law keeps the money already paid.
consequences.city_reaction: At least the new chairs only cost half as much. Progress. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Defend the purchases as premium quality necessary for civic dignity.

consequences.follow_up: The mayor gives a press conference sitting in a luxury chair. It breaks on live television.
consequences.city_reaction: Civic dignity looking real fragile right now. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Auction off all the overpriced furniture to recoup costs.

consequences.follow_up: Wealthy citizens buy the items as joke pieces. City officials are left sitting on milk crates for weeks.
consequences.city_reaction: I bought the mayor's desk for my cat. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Disappearing Relief in {city.name}
trigger: An international NGO demands to know what happened to a $10 million grant meant for {city.primary_industry_workers}.
description: The funds were supposed to build affordable housing near {city.landmark}. Instead the money has been routed through shell accounts, and the housing site is currently a luxury golf course under construction.

options.label: Reallocate city funds to build a scaled-down housing project immediately.

consequences.follow_up: The city goes broke fulfilling the grant requirements. The housing is built but barely adequate.
consequences.city_reaction: They built us sheds and kept the golf course. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Claim the golf course is actually green infrastructure for flood management.

consequences.follow_up: The NGO publicly blacklists the city. The mayor is photographed playing 18 holes on the flood management site.
consequences.city_reaction: Glad my tax dollars are working on their short game. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Seize the golf course via eminent domain and open it free to the public.

consequences.follow_up: The developers sue the city for billions. Citizens use the sand traps as impromptu volleyball courts.
consequences.city_reaction: Finally, a place to walk my dog without a leash. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Family Business in {city.name}
trigger: It is revealed that the city's top efficiency consultant is a 22-year-old relative of a powerful official.
description: The consultant was paid a six-figure retainer to produce a report on optimizing the {city.government_body}. The report was a printed Wikipedia article on Management, complete with blue hyperlinks still visible.

options.label: Quietly terminate the contract and bury the report.

consequences.follow_up: The kid keeps the money but the contract ends. The official grumbles but accepts the compromise.
consequences.city_reaction: Wish my uncle could get me a fake job paying six figures. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Force the consultant to present their findings publicly.

consequences.follow_up: The presentation is a humiliating disaster. The kid cries on live TV. The official vows revenge.
consequences.city_reaction: Most entertaining city council meeting of the year. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Implement the Wikipedia recommendations verbatim across all departments.

consequences.follow_up: City hall is reorganized based on a misread paragraph about synergy. Total bureaucratic paralysis for weeks.
consequences.city_reaction: Nobody knows what their job is anymore. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Up in Smoke in {city.name}
trigger: The night before a massive federal audit, the municipal archives building mysteriously burns to the ground.
description: The fire chief notes the blaze started simultaneously in three filing cabinets containing records for {city.landmark} construction. {city.media_outlet} implies arson, but the {city.government_body} calls it a tragic electrical anomaly.

options.label: Cooperate fully with auditors using whatever digital backups remain.

consequences.follow_up: The digital backups are conveniently corrupted, but the gesture of cooperation slightly appeases the feds.
consequences.city_reaction: The USB drive fell in a blender? What are the odds. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Blame the fire on a marginalized activist group.

consequences.follow_up: The public doesn't buy it. Protests erupt and the activists sue the city for defamation.
consequences.city_reaction: They think we're stupid. We are, but not that stupid. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Claim the destroyed documents proved the city is owed money by the federal government.

consequences.follow_up: The audacious lie confuses the auditors so much it delays the investigation by years. The feds are furious.
consequences.city_reaction: Gaslighting the federal government. You love to see it. {city.slang_reaction}.
